text: Nausea is common in the first trimester. Try small, frequent meals instead of three large ones, and keep plain crackers by your bed to eat before getting up.
A banana or a handful of almonds makes a gentle mid-morning snack that's easy on an unsettled stomach.
Ginger tea, taken warm and slowly, is a gentle traditional remedy many mothers find helpful for morning queasiness.
Add a serving of lentils or beans to one meal today -- they're a good plant-based source of iron and protein for this stage of pregnancy.
Pair an iron-rich food with something high in vitamin C today, like spinach with a squeeze of lemon, to help your body absorb the iron better.
A boiled egg is a simple, protein-rich snack that travels well if you're out for antenatal checkups today.
A glass of milk or a bowl of yogurt today helps toward your calcium needs for the baby's developing bones.
Smaller, more frequent meals can help with the fullness and heartburn that often show up in the third trimester as the baby takes up more room.
Fiber-rich foods like whole grains, guava, or papaya can help with constipation, which is common as pregnancy progresses.
Keep fruit portions moderate and spread through the day rather than all at once, since blood sugar can be more sensitive in the third trimester.
A small piece of grilled fish or chicken today is a good source of protein to support the baby's continued growth.
Try to drink water steadily through the day rather than large amounts at once -- it's gentler on digestion and helps with energy levels.
If you haven't already, ask your doctor whether your iron and folic acid supplement dose is still right for this stage.
Early pregnancy can bring a mix of excitement and worry. Take five quiet minutes today just to notice how you're actually feeling, without judging it.
Tell one person you trust something small about how you're feeling today -- it doesn't have to be a big conversation.
Write down one thing today that made you smile, however small. Small positive moments add up over the weeks.
Try three slow, deep breaths right now -- in for four counts, out for six. A tiny reset can help if today feels like a lot.
If you can, step outside for a few minutes today, even just to sit. Natural light and fresh air can gently lift mood.
It's normal to feel a mix of anticipation and nervousness as your due date gets closer. Naming that feeling out loud to someone can make it lighter.
If sleep is harder to come by right now, resting quietly even without sleeping still counts as rest -- try not to add extra pressure on yourself about it.
Check in with yourself for a moment: on a scale of 1 to 5, how are you feeling today? Logging it in your Mood Check-in only takes a few seconds.
If worry or low mood has been sticking around for more than a couple of weeks, it's worth mentioning to your doctor -- this is common, and support helps.
A slow 10-minute walk today, at whatever pace feels comfortable, is a gentle way to stay active in early pregnancy.
Try a short set of gentle shoulder rolls and ankle circles today -- small movements that ease stiffness without much effort.
If you enjoy it, a short prenatal yoga or stretching session today can help with posture as your center of balance shifts.
Gentle pelvic tilts today may help ease lower back discomfort, which tends to increase in the third trimester.
A short, slow walk today can help with circulation and swelling, as long as it feels comfortable -- stop and rest if you feel tired.
Whatever movement you choose today, listen to your body first -- comfortable and steady matters more than intensity.
If you notice heavy bleeding or severe cramping, contact your doctor promptly rather than waiting for your next scheduled visit.
Keep track of your baby's movements as they become noticeable -- a clear change in pattern is worth mentioning to your doctor.
Severe headache, blurred vision, or sudden swelling in the face and hands need prompt medical attention -- these can be signs that need urgent checking.
If your baby's movements feel noticeably reduced today, contact your doctor rather than waiting to see if it changes.
